summary_one_line: Input must form a well-formed URL/protocol header so multi_done runs with result==0 and a nonzero progress-callback rc.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured request whose leading bytes spell a recognizable scheme/host/port and HTTP token (e.g. the 'pop3'/'9001'/'HTTP/' literals at offsets 0x06-0x2b) so the transfer reaches multi_done with result==0 and a nonzero Curl_pgrsDone rc, taking the true branch at multi.c:654.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: vpc winning seeds carry concentrated structural literals — '/:::' run at 0x0b-0x10, '9001' at 0x17-0x1a, and the 'GHTTP/ m' token at 0x24-0x2b — that the cmplog I2S stage substituted to satisfy the scheme/header comparisons, driving execution down to the blocker with !result true and rc nonzero (winner avg hitcount on branch 1674).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are short, '%'-padded noise (e.g. 'CdUC%%%%!%e', './/././') lacking the matching scheme/port/HTTP literals; they never reach a state where multi_done sees result==0 with a nonzero rc, so they stay on the false side (loser avg hitcount 1).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share value_profile's comparison-distance feedback; the only technique delta is vpc's I2SRandReplace, which copies the parser's expected comparison operands directly into the input, manufacturing the exact scheme/port/HTTP-token bytes that the distance feedback alone could not climb to within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of if(!result && rc) at multi.c:654, globally blocked on the T side.
The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile with technique delta I2S.
The winner resolves all 10 trials with high branch hitcount while the loser stays blocked at hitcount 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1674  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds carry structured scheme/port/HTTP literals such as pop3, 9001 and HTTP/.
Winner seeds concentrate the GHTTP/ token at offsets around 0x24.
Loser-blocking seeds are short percent-padded noise lacking the structural literals.
At the blocker line the winner takes T=10/F=0 while the loser takes T=0/F=10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 654  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  if(!result && rc)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The byte-diff shows the winner has a concentrated HTTP-token block at 0x24-0x2b absent in the loser.
The only mutator-level difference between the two involved fuzzers is that vpc adds I2SRandReplace.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so its only comparison aid is the CMP_MAP gradient.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 297 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0057778b67d14b3b`; the 25-level ancestor chain contains 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 23 and 24, the characteristic unwrapped-I2SRandReplace signal exclusive to cmplog/vpc.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the scheme/port/HTTP literals gating multi_done are made byte-derivable by a Hamming gradient (no exact-match cliff) and value_profile then resolves the branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog, showing the divergence was CMP_MAP-solvable rather than I2S-dependent.

weakest_evidence_point: The source line if(!result && rc) is a generic post-transfer guard, so the specific scheme/port literals being the gate is inferred from seed bytes and call-chain divergence rather than from the CMP at the blocker itself.
